hypothesis: If property-specific steering vectors learned by a concept bottleneck model are added to a frozen LLM’s intermediate activations during molecular generation, then the model will more accurately and efficiently match specified molecular property targets (e.g., QED, LogP) while maintaining validity, novelty, and synthesizability—and will outperform vanilla generation, ActAdd, and fine-tuning baselines across in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings—because the learned, orthogonalized concept vectors provide disentangled linear control over property-relevant directions in the representation space.
